questions.question: Define structure and union in C. Explain the key differences between them with respect to memory allocation.
keyPoints: Structure: user-defined type grouping different data types, each member gets separate memory
Union: user-defined type where all members share the same memory location
Structure size >= sum of all member sizes; Union size = size of largest member
All structure members can hold values simultaneously; only one union member at a time
Example declarations and memory layout diagrams for both
Use of structure and union in real-world applications

questions.question: Explain self-referential structures with an example. How are they used in implementing linked lists?
keyPoints: Self-referential structure contains a pointer to a variable of the same structure type
Example: struct Node { int data; struct Node *next; };
The pointer member allows each node to point to the next node, forming a chain
This is the foundation for linked lists, trees, and graphs
Diagram showing nodes connected through pointers
Memory allocation and deallocation for linked lists

questions.question: Explain how to access structure members using the dot (.) and arrow (->) operators with examples.
keyPoints: Dot operator (.) used with structure variables: student.name
Arrow operator (->) used with pointers to structures: ptr->name
ptr->member is equivalent to (*ptr).member
Code examples demonstrating both operators
Common mistakes when using dot and arrow operators

questions.question: What is typedef? Explain its usage with structures with an example.
keyPoints: typedef creates an alias (alternative name) for a data type
Simplifies structure declarations by eliminating the need to write 'struct' keyword each time
Example: typedef struct { int x; int y; } Point;
After typedef, declare variables as: Point p1; instead of struct Point p1;
Benefits of using typedef in large programs

questions.question: Write a C program to demonstrate the use of an array of structures to store and display details of 5 students.
keyPoints: Define a Student structure with fields like name, roll number, marks
Declare an array of 5 Student structures
Use a loop to input details for each student
Use another loop to display all student details
Proper use of dot operator to access members
Error handling for invalid inputs

questions.question: Explain the concept of structure padding and alignment. How does it affect the size of a structure?
keyPoints: Structure padding: inserting bytes to align members to memory boundaries
Structure alignment: aligning the entire structure to a memory boundary
Effect on structure size: increased size due to padding
Example: struct { char c; int i; };
Calculating the size of a structure considering padding

questions.question: Compare and contrast structures and unions in C. Provide examples to illustrate their differences.
keyPoints: Similarities: both are user-defined types, can contain multiple members
Differences: memory allocation, access, and usage
Structure: each member has separate memory, all members can be accessed simultaneously
Union: all members share the same memory, only one member can be accessed at a time
Example code demonstrating the differences
